name: Russian Empire
note: Peter the Great declared the Russian Empire in 1721 after the Great Northern War victory over Sweden. Peter's Westernisation programme transformed Russia into a European Great Power. Catherine the Great (1762–1796) expanded the empire south (Crimea) and west (Poland partitions). 19th-century Russia was the largest state in the world by land area. Defeated in Crimea (1853–56) and Japan (1904–5); autocracy resisted reform until too late; collapsed in Revolution 1917.

figures.name: Peter the Great
figures.significance: Transformed Russia from an isolated Muscovite tsardom into a European Great Power; founded St Petersburg; built the Russian navy; defeated Sweden at Poltava; declared the Russian Empire

figures.name: Catherine the Great
figures.significance: Greatest Russian tsarina; conquered Crimea; participated in the three Polish partitions; corresponded with Voltaire and Diderot; patron of the Enlightenment while ruling autocratically

figures.name: Nicholas II
figures.significance: Last tsar; stubborn defender of autocracy against all reform; Russia's defeats in WWI triggered the Revolutions of 1917; abdicated February 1917; executed with his family by Bolsheviks July 1918
